page: Amazon Web Services c8a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU architecture includes a 32 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 10% of tested instances. Single-threaded operations like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also achieve top-tier throughput. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core metrics, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth for both read and write operations on large blocks is average, while static web serving throughput and image processing show strong performance in the top 25%. For machine learning workloads, the server delivers top-tier text generation speeds for small 135M parameter LLM models, though performance drops to average for medium 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8a.2xlarge offers high-density compute resources with dedicated AMD EPYC cores and DDR5 memory, making it a viable option for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. The baseline network bandwidth is rated at 4 Gbps. This hardware profile is well-suited for workloads such as single-threaded data processing, web serving, software compilation, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but features a 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong static web serving and small-model LLM inference capabilities. Multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. This configuration is highly suited for compute-intensive, single-threaded tasks, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference where high-speed dedicated CPU cores are required without massive memory footprints.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC cores at 4.5 GHz, 16.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 8 AMD EPYC cores, 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring dedicated high-frequency AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 memory for single-threaded and web-serving workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (2.0 GB per core)
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for web serving, software compilation, and light LLM inference
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
